seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in Vercel AI SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in Vercel AI SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Vercel AI SDK agents, tool loops manifest as infinite or excessively long cycles where the agent repeatedly calls the same tools like `search` or `calculate` without progressing toward a final answer, often due to unhandled edge cases in tool responses or missing loop-breaking logic in `generateText` streams. This is exacerbated by the SDK's streaming nature via `streamText`, where partial tool calls accumulate without validation, leading to high token usage and timeouts in Vercel Edge functions. Developers commonly overlook loop detection in agent patterns using `useChat` or custom tool chains, resulting in unresponsive UIs and escalated costs.

howItManifests.scenario: Recursive math solver agent calling `calculate` tool on its own output
howItManifests.symptom: StreamText output shows repeating 'Calling calculate...' tokens without resolution
howItManifests.impact: Edge function hits 30s timeout, user sees frozen spinner in React UI

howItManifests.symptom: Tool call history in `useChat` grows >50 calls with identical queries
howItManifests.impact: Token costs spike 10x, inconsistent structured output across OpenAI/Anthropic

howItManifests.symptom: Partial hallucinations in stream like 'Weather in [location] requires location... Calling getLocation...'
howItManifests.impact: Broken streaming UI with unterminated tool call spinners

howItManifests.scenario: Custom agent with stateful tools lacking convergence criteria
howItManifests.symptom: generateText loop exceeds 100 iterations per `streamText` call
howItManifests.impact: High Vercel function invocation costs and poor user experience

fixes.description: Implements stream timeout with emergency final answer

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates tool loop detection by injecting middleware into Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` and `streamText` calls, monitoring tool call patterns, iteration counts, and token velocity in real-time across deployments. It captures loop signatures (toolName+args hashes) and alerts teams via Slack/webhooks when cycles exceed configurable thresholds, preventing production incidents. Vex's agent observability traces full tool call graphs, automatically suggesting loop-breaking system prompts and maxIteration limits based on historical patterns.

Integration requires one line: wrap your AI SDK calls with `vex.middleware()`. Vex handles Edge function timeouts by force-terminating loops and injecting fallback responses, maintaining UI responsiveness. Remediation is automatic—Vex can deploy hotfixes updating `maxIterations` and tool schemas across your Vercel deployments.

For complex multi-tool agents, Vex builds dependency graphs showing `search->summarize->search` cycles and recommends convergence tools like 'execute' or 'finalize'. This reduces MTTR from hours to seconds while cutting token waste by 70%+.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `vex.middleware()` to your Vercel AI SDK routes and eliminate tool loops forever.
